title: Quiz: Barren Plateau Mitigation Strategies
description: Test your understanding of strategies to mitigate barren plateaus

questions.question: What is identity initialization for variational circuits?

options.text: Setting all parameters to random values between 0 and 2π
Initializing parameters so the circuit approximates the identity transformation
Copying parameters from a pretrained model
Setting parameters equal to the number of qubits

questions.explanation: Identity initialization sets parameters near zero so that U(θ₀) ≈ I. This keeps the initial state close to the input, preserving gradient signal.

questions.question: How does layer-wise training help avoid barren plateaus?

options.text: It increases the number of parameters
It builds circuit depth gradually, preventing immediate gradient vanishing

questions.explanation: Layer-wise training starts with shallow circuits (good gradients) and adds layers after previous ones converge, avoiding the immediate barren plateau of deep circuits.

questions.question: Why do local cost functions help with barren plateaus?

options.text: They reduce the number of measurements needed
They have polynomially decaying gradients instead of exponentially decaying
They remove the need for entanglement

questions.explanation: Local cost functions (measuring few qubits) have gradient variance that decays only polynomially with system size, not exponentially like global costs.

questions.question: Using correlated parameters (same angle for multiple gates) reduces the effective dimension and can help with trainability.
questions.explanation: Parameter correlation reduces the number of independent parameters, which decreases expressibility but improves trainability by avoiding barren plateaus.

questions.question: What is a typical scale for identity initialization?
questions.explanation: Small random values around zero (scale ~0.01) create near-identity circuits while breaking symmetry to enable optimization.

questions.question: In layer-wise training, when should a new layer be added?

options.text: After the previous layer's parameters have converged
When gradient variance is at its lowest

questions.explanation: New layers should be added after existing layers are trained (converged), ensuring the circuit maintains good gradient signal as depth increases.

questions.question: What is a drawback of using highly correlated parameters?
questions.explanation: Parameter correlation reduces the number of independent parameters, which limits the range of functions the circuit can express (lower expressibility).

questions.question: Which combinations of strategies are commonly used together? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Effective combinations include identity init with layer-wise training (a), local costs with shallow circuits (b), and correlation with identity init (d). Random init with deep circuits (c) leads to barren plateaus.
